zh.Carmarthenshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip於1380年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾聖 可利阿斯時，他的父親Lord Philip Ap Madog30歲，母親Ellen Verch Gwilym35歲。他於1411年與Gwendolena Verch Evan在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1442年在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip于1380年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾聖 可利阿斯时，他的父亲Lord Philip Ap Madog30岁，母亲Ellen Verch Gwilym35岁。他于1411年与Gwendolena Verch Evan在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1442年在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Philipps
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, North German, and Dutch: variant of   Phillips  .

variants.name: Phillips
meanings: English, Dutch, North German, and Jewish (western Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name   Philip  . In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages (see   Philips  ).

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

variants.name: McKillip
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’ (see Philip ). See also McPhillips .

variants.name: Felipe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Juana, Miguel, Orlando, Pedro, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Jesus.
Spanish: from the personal name Felipe, an equivalent of Philippe .

variants.name: Lips
meanings: North German, Flemish, and Dutch: from a shortened form of the personal name Philips, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Lipps
meanings: North German: from a shortened form of the personal name Philipps, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Pepin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Camille, Emile, Pierre, Gilles, Lucien, Normand, Alcide, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Pépin) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Pipin, of uncertain, probably onomatopoeic origin. The name was borne by several Frankish kings, most notably Pepin le Bref, father of Charlemagne, and remained popular throughout the early Middle Ages.
English: from the unrecorded Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal names Pipin, Pepin, Pipun, pet forms of Philip . Compare Pipe .

variants.name: Lippens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alfons, Alphons.  French Andre.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from a shortened form of the personal name   Philip  .

variants.name: Philbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Philbín, ‘son of Philbin (Irish Philbín or Filibín)’, a pet form of Philip .

variants.name: Lipman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mayer, Chaim, Dov, Isadore, Jakob, Meyer, Pinchas.

Dutch: habitational name from a farm named Lipman or perhaps Lippink, referring to a certain Lippo and his kin. Lippo is a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name composed with the element leiba ‘offspring, son’, or of the personal name Philip .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Lipman, derived from Middle High German words liep ‘dear, beloved’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Philipose
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Philipose, derived from Greek Philippos (see Philip ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Philibert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Fernand, Mirielle, Pascale, Pierrot.

French: from the personal name Philibert, from ancient Germanic Filuberht, composed of the elements fila ‘much, very’ + berht ‘bright’, which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, abbot of Jumièges. The spelling has been influenced by personal names of Greek origin based on philein ‘to love’, e.g. Philip .

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip was born in 1380, in St Clears, Carmarthenshire, Wales, his father, Lord Philip Ap Madog, was 30 and his mother, Ellen Verch Gwilym, was 35. He married Gwendolena Verch Evan in 1411, in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1442, in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 62.

fr.of Hirfryn,Cantref Bychan,Carmarthenshir: Cantref, Breconshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip est né en 1380, à St Clears, Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, Lord Philip Ap Madog, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ellen Verch Gwilym, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Gwendolena Verch Evan en 1411, à Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1442, à Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip 1380 in St Clears, Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Philip Ap Madog 30 und seine Mutter Ellen Verch Gwilym 35. Er hat 1411 in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom Gwendolena Verch Evan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1442 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip è nato il nel 1380 nel St Clears, Carmarthenshire, Galles, il suo padre, Lord Philip Ap Madog, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Ellen Verch Gwilym, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Gwendolena Verch Evan nel 1411, nel Carmarthenshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1442, nel Carmarthenshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip(이)가 1380년 웨일스 카마테실르 스트 클리르스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Philip Ap Madog(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen Verch Gwilym(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1411년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르에서 Gwendolena Verch Evan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1442년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip родился в 1380 г. в местности ст клирс, кармартеншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Lord Philip Ap Madog было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellen Verch Gwilym, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwendolena Verch Evan в 1411 г. в местности кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1442 г. в местности кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Maredudd Philipps ap Philip nació en 1380, en St Clears, Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, Lord Philip Ap Madog, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Ellen Verch Gwilym, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Gwendolena Verch Evan en 1411, en Carmarthenshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1442, en Carmarthenshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años.
